name: Kingsport Press Cu
description_short: Kingsport Press Credit Union is a Tennessee-based credit union established in 1930, serving 6,900+ members with $83.6M in assets across 1 branch and 3 ATMs.
description_long:
Kingsport Press Credit Union was established in 1930 and is headquartered in Kingsport, Tennessee. As a state-chartered credit union, it has grown to serve over 6,900 loyal members while stewarding $83.6 million in assets. The organization maintains a modest physical footprint with 1 main branch located at 528 W Center Street and 3 ATMs, supported by a dedicated workforce of over 21 employees.

Kingsport Press offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services typical of mid-sized credit unions. Members can access checking accounts (Share Draft accounts with 3,700+ holders), savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (400+ members), credit cards, auto loans for new and used vehicles, personal loans, mortgage products including fixed-rate and adjustable-rate options, IRAs/KEOGH accounts, and online banking services. Additional services include bill pay, e-statements, wire transfers, online loan payments, and financial counseling and education programs.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its long institutional history dating back nearly a century, its focus on community engagement through student scholarships and financial education, and its comprehensive digital banking capabilities including mobile apps for both iOS and Android. The organization provides multiple loan calculators (mortgage, auto, and credit card payoff) and maintains an active online presence with service status monitoring and community Q&A forums for member support.

As a traditional community credit union, Kingsport Press is best suited for Tennessee residents seeking straightforward banking products with personalized service. The main limitation is its single physical branch location and limited ATM network, which may inconvenience members outside the immediate Kingsport area. The credit union's asset size and member base position it as a stable but modest financial institution without the technological sophistication or product breadth of larger regional or national alternatives.
pros: Established in 1930 with nearly 95 years of institutional history and stability
Serves 6,900+ members with $83.6M in assets, indicating solid financial health
Offers comprehensive product suite including mortgages, auto loans, credit cards, and investment accounts
Provides mobile banking apps for both iOS and Android with online bill pay and e-statements
Includes financial counseling, financial education, and student scholarship programs for community support
State-chartered credit union with transparent service status monitoring and community Q&A forums
Multiple loan calculators available online for mortgage, auto, and credit card planning
cons: Only 1 physical branch location in Kingsport, TN with limited geographic reach
Minimal ATM network with just 3 ATMs, inconvenient for members outside immediate area
Lobby currently closed according to website (operational status unclear)
No online reviews available yet, limiting member feedback and reputation visibility
Limited to Tennessee state charter, restricting membership eligibility to state residents
best_for: Tennessee residents in or near Kingsport seeking stable community banking relationships
Members prioritizing comprehensive loan products including mortgages and auto financing
Credit union members wanting financial education and counseling services
Individuals seeking a traditional credit union with long-established history and stability
diagnosis: Kingsport Press Credit Union is best for Tennessee residents who value personalized service and a stable, traditional credit union with comprehensive lending products and financial education. The primary caveat is the single-branch, limited-ATM footprint, which makes it less practical for members who need convenient physical access outside the immediate Kingsport area or prefer financial institutions with broader geographic reach.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
